channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-Iran Tensions Lead to Hormuz Closure Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for oil and gas shipments from the Gulf, remains closed as Iran's Revolutionary Guards demand the US end its 'acts of aggression'. The conflict, which began in late...

item.title: Toronto's population decline: A crisis of urban governance and affordability
item.description: A recent analysis by the Toronto Metropolitan University reveals a significant exodus from Toronto, with residents citing systemic issues such as inadequate public transit, unaffordable housing, risin...

item.title: IBM Faces Financial Challenges Amid Profit Warnings
item.description: IBM, a leading technology company, recently issued a profit warning that has raised concerns among investors and analysts. The warning, which highlights declining revenues and increased operational co...

item.title: Three Suspects Arrested in Doctor's Robbery and Murder Case in Karachi
item.description: Karachi police have arrested three suspects allegedly involved in the fatal shooting of a young doctor during a robbery near Teen Talwar in Clifton. The 28-year-old doctor was killed during a robbery ...

item.title: Verification of Mitch McConnell's 'Proof of Life' Photo and Health Rumors
item.description: The article discusses the verification of Senator Mitch McConnell's 'proof of life' photo, which sparked conspiracy theories about his health. An intern at the Washington Post investigated the image, ...

item.title: AI Tools Transform Farming Practices in Canada Through Data-Driven Insights
item.description: Canadian agtech startup CropMind is leveraging artificial intelligence to revolutionize farming practices, helping growers make data-driven decisions and reduce uncertainty. The cloud-based SaaS platf...

item.title: Camila Alves: A Look at Her Career as a Model and Entrepreneur
item.description: Camila Alves, wife of actor Matthew McConaughey, is a Brazilian-American entrepreneur, model, designer, author, and philanthropist. Born in Brazil, she moved to the U.S. at 15, working odd jobs before...

item.title: Edmonton Councillor Michael Janz Faces Concerns Over Social Media Posts on Gaza Conflict
item.description: Edmonton city councillor Michael Janz has faced criticism from the Jewish Federation of Edmonton for sharing pro-Palestine content on his Instagram stories, which some argue spreads misinformation abo...

item.title: Tanzania's Tourism Sector Surpasses $4.41 Billion in 2025, Driven by Increased Visitor Spending
item.description: Tanzania's tourism sector reached a historic milestone in 2025, generating $4.41 billion in revenue, driven by higher visitor spending that outpaced growth in international arrivals. The country's tou...

item.title: Lagos Launches Tree Planting Initiative to Combat Climate Change and Urbanization
item.description: The Lagos State Governor, Babatunde Sanwo-Olu, has launched a comprehensive tree planting initiative aimed at addressing climate change, urbanization challenges, and environmental degradation. The pro...

item.title: Supreme Court allows Muslim prayers near disputed Bhojshala site after rejecting restoration of Friday namaz
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has issued notices on petitions challenging the Madhya Pradesh High Court's ruling that declared the Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque complex as a Hindu temple. The court declin...

item.title: Addressing Delays in the Madlanga Inquiry to Ensure Justice
item.description: The article discusses the frustration caused by repeated delays in the Madlanga commission of inquiry, which is tasked with uncovering corruption and restoring public confidence. Justice Mbuyiseli Mad...

item.title: Filipinos Prioritize AFP and PCG Modernization for Maritime Defense
item.description: A recent Social Weather Stations (SWS) survey commissioned by the Stratbase Institute reveals that Filipinos prioritize modernizing the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and the Philippine Coast G...

item.title: Card Delinquencies and Their Impact on Major Banks
item.description: The article discusses the discrepancy between reported card delinquencies and the lack of visible impacts on major banks. Despite rising consumer debt and economic uncertainties, large financial insti...

item.title: Mamelodi Police Arrest Two Suspects in Electricity Extortion Case
item.description: In Mamelodi, police have arrested two men accused of extorting households by demanding payments for illegal electricity connections. The operation began after complaints from residents in Selahliwe ab...

item.title: US confirms limited Nvidia H200 AI chip shipments to China amid tech restrictions
item.description: A US official revealed that a small number of Nvidia's H200 AI chips, one of the company's most powerful offerings, have been shipped to China. Jeffrey Kessler, under secretary of commerce for industr...

item.title: Politician faces backlash after alleged political retaliation for police misconduct allegations
item.description: The article details Moira Deeming's public dispute with the Victorian Liberal Party over their decision to distance themselves from her following a police complaint. Deeming claims the party is retali...

item.title: IEBC Warns Against Govt Officials Interfering in Ol Kalou Polling Stations
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has issued a stern warning to government officials and politicians against accessing polling stations during the Ol Kalou by-election. Commis...

item.title: Security forces intensify operations against terrorists in Balochistan during Operation Shaban
item.description: Operation Shaban, launched in retaliation for a deadly attack on a police post at the Mangi Dam pumping station in Ziarat, has resulted in the killing of 88 terrorists as security forces continue join...

item.title: AI Research Uncovers Complex Factors Behind Radicalization
item.description: Mijke van den Hurk, an AI scientist, investigated how artificial intelligence could help unravel the intricate factors driving radicalization. Her Ph.D. research, conducted in collaboration with the D...
